heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
Each module has predefined numeric thresholds for hypothesis testing:
- EEG: δ = 0.05, α = 0.001, Cohen’s d ≥ 0.5, minimum power ≥ 0.90, N ≥ 161
- HRV: |r| > 0.3, α = 0.001, power ≥ 0.90, N = TBD
- Olfaction: AUC ≥ 0.75, α = 0.001, power ≥ 0.95, N = TBD
Any result failing to meet these thresholds will be considered non-significant, regardless of exploratory findings.

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body:
The full study protocol will be pre-registered on the Open Science Framework (OSF) before data collection begins.
- OSF ID: osf.io/TBD (placeholder)
- Planned registration date: 2026-06-01 (or earlier, before first participant enrollment)
- Contents: primary hypotheses, outcome measures, analysis plan, exclusion criteria, and power analysis.

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
Sample size is calculated using the standard formula for a two-sided test:
n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · σ² / δ²
where:
- Z_α/2 = 1.96 (α = 0.05, two-tailed)
- Z_β = 0.84 (power = 0.80)
- σ² = TBD (estimated from pilot data or literature)
- δ = TBD (minimum clinically meaningful effect size)
For EEG module, the required total N is 161 (per-group N ≈ 54) based on Cohen’s d ≥ 0.5. For HRV and olfaction, N will be determined after pilot data collection.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Hardware failure (EEG dry electrodes) | Medium | High | Redundant sensors; daily calibration checks |
| Low participant recruitment | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment; extended enrollment period |
| Signal noise from motion artifacts | High | Medium | Adaptive filtering; exclusion criteria for excessive motion |
| Olfactory sensor cross-reactivity | Medium | Medium | Reference gas calibration; machine learning classification |
| Data privacy breach | Low | Critical | Encryption; anonymization; IRB-approved data handling |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- The Ze-theory-based biomarker (χ_Ze) has been validated only on EEG data; HRV and olfaction modules lack independent validation.
- Sample size for EEG validation (N=161) is a placeholder; actual power may differ after pilot data.
- Olfactory module relies on theoretical electron tunneling (Turing model) which has not been empirically confirmed for human volatiles.
- All modules assume stationary signal conditions; real-world ambulatory use may introduce unmodeled artifacts.
- The fixed theoretical point v* = 0.45631 is derived from a dynamical systems model; its biological universality is assumed but not proven across all populations.
